validated_tests.evm_cladapter_redirect_exploit: PASS: NSS: CLAdapter cached payload redirects a later CCIP lock to an attacker payload Artifact in tests/Bridge.ts 'With Chainlink Adapter' describe. seed=signer1 deposits 50_000_000 of lbtc through bridgeSource to attackerReceiver (rate=10% commission -> seededNetAmount=45_000_000). 1 follow-up lockOrBurn from onRamp impersonating router returns destPoolData=sha256(seeded.payload), emits LockedOrBurned(amount=45_000_000), drains ccipAmount-seededNetAmount=55_000_000 from aTokenPool into aCLAdapter (stuck), and orphan victim's receiver on chain B (rewards attacker instead). Full set: 21 passing (2s) across nss/PropEvmCrossLayerDivergence and Bridge suites; redirect exploitable in legacy Chainlink adapter substrate. Evidence: evidence/evm-cladapter-redirect-clean.log, evidence/evm-cladapter-bridge-fullset.log, evidence/evm-prop-xr-final.log.
validated_tests.evm_partner_bridge_b_greater_a_honest_ze: PASS: PROP-XR-EVM-011B test 'BridgeV2._withdraw always mints amount from source-side msgBody — B>A cannot occur' inside nss/PropEvmCrossLayerDivergence.ts. The destination BridgeV2 mint amount is bound to source-side encoded msgBody amount set by the source chain at deposit time. Without source-side compromise, B cannot exceed A. Closes v6.51.13 PROP-XR-EVM-011's open side: destinationAmount=A and mint=B can never diverge. Evidence: evidence/evm-prop-011b-partner-bridge.log, evidence/evm-prop-xr-r2-final.log. Full set: 22 passing (2s) including this assertion.

Evidence: evidence/evm-fork-mainnet-probe.log.

new_signals.summary: release_or_mint_tokens calls mailbox.handle_message with handler=token-pool state PDA; pool_signer is a remaining account for bridge.gmp_receive. Therefore destination_caller must equal state PDA bytes, not pool_signer bytes.

Mailbox marks message_info Handled before recipient CPI, but validator replay N4 confirms Solana transaction atomicity rolls back this write when downstream release_or_mint_tokens / bridge.gmp_receive path reverts. Post-state remains MessageState::Delivered (byte 1), not Handled (byte 2).

EVM Mailbox._deliverAndHandle wraps IHandler.handlePayload in try/catch and only sets handledPayload[payloadHash]=true on success; handler revert does NOT bubble and message stays Delivered / re-attemptable. Solana mailbox.handle_message writes Handled then invoke_signed, Anchor transaction atomicity rolls back Handled write on recipient CPI revert. Both gates dedupe via PDA init keyed on [MESSAGE_SEED, payload_hash]. Net effect: Solana requires a fresh tx after a failed gmp_receive; EVM allows re-deliverAndHandle on the same Delivered payload.

post_session_signatures indexes current_validators[index as usize] / current_weights[index as usize] / session.signed[index as usize] without bounds checking. Any attacker-supplied index >= current_validators.len() panics in pure Rust; on Solana, it surfaces as an unrecoverable transaction failure. Reachable impact is session DoS (no fund-loss CPI before panic). Rust probe in programs/consortium/src/utils/post_session_signatures_probe.rs (3 tests pass). Fix: add require!(*index < current_validators.len() as u64, ConsortiumError::ValidatorIndexOutOfBounds).

Crucible scaffold dry-run for lombard_token_pool is now PASSING after copying the mainnet-feature-built lombard_token_pool.so (686 KB) into the scaffold target/deploy/. Crucible reported: DRY-RUN Harness Validation passed! - 1 tracked accounts - 1 programs loaded - Single iteration completed successfully. Stateful 5s smoke ran 4 iterations with 0 crashes.
Anchor TS aggregate run showed 16 before-all failures from shared-PDA cross-pollution; per-file isolation (anchor-test-each.sh) confirms these are test-infra, not protocol bugs.

Continue R4 (Crucible stateful R7 actions) in a fresh session to extend the multi-program scaffold with three typed sister-program actions.
